zotero.abstractNote: Reflectometry is a powerful technique for characterizing thin-film structures, but the inversion of reflectometry data—inferring the physical parameters of a sample from its reflectivity curve—is a notoriously difficult inverse problem. It is often ill-posed, computationally expensive, and prone to local minima. We present a method for probabilistic reflectometry inversion based on prior-amortized neural posterior estimation (NPE). Our approach learns to map reflectivity curves to the full posterior distribution of physical parameters, conditioned on a prior. Once trained, the model provides instantaneous, reliable, and uncertainty-aware parameter estimation. We demonstrate the performance of our method on synthetic and experimental data, showing that it outperforms traditional optimization-based approaches in terms of speed, robustness, and the ability to quantify uncertainty. Our method is general and can be applied to various reflectometry modalities and complex sample architectures.
